title: Azorella crenata

This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and its relationship to other plants in the same family. The exact classification of Azorella crenata is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Apiales, family Apiaceae, genus Azorella, and species A. crenata.

intro: Azorella crenata, also known as Creeping Snowberry, is a small, flowering plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Azorella crenata is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Azorella crenata include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Azorella crenata is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is traditionally used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
